name: auth
title: Auth
description: A Better Auth foundation for TanStack Start with Drizzle PostgreSQL tables, server config, React client, and an /api/auth route handler.

files.content:
import { createAuthClient } from "better-auth/react";

export const authClient = createAuthClient();

import { pgTable, text, timestamp, boolean, index } from "drizzle-orm/pg-core";

export const user = pgTable("user", {
  id: text("id").primaryKey(),
  name: text("name").notNull(),
  email: text("email").notNull().unique(),
  emailVerified: boolean("email_verified").default(false).notNull(),
  image: text("image"),
  createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow().notNull(),
  updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at")
    .defaultNow()
    .$onUpdate(() => /* @__PURE__ */ new Date())
    .notNull(),
});

export const session = pgTable(
  "session",
  {
    id: text("id").primaryKey(),
    expiresAt: timestamp("expires_at").notNull(),
    token: text("token").notNull().unique(),
    createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow().notNull(),
    updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at")
      .$onUpdate(() => /* @__PURE__ */ new Date())
      .notNull(),
    ipAddress: text("ip_address"),
    userAgent: text("user_agent"),
    userId: text("user_id")
      .notNull()
      .references(() => user.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
  },
  (table) => [index("session_userId_idx").on(table.userId)],
);

export const account = pgTable(
  "account",
  {
    id: text("id").primaryKey(),
    accountId: text("account_id").notNull(),
    providerId: text("provider_id").notNull(),
    userId: text("user_id")
      .notNull()
      .references(() => user.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
    accessToken: text("access_token"),
    refreshToken: text("refresh_token"),
    idToken: text("id_token"),
    accessTokenExpiresAt: timestamp("access_token_expires_at"),
    refreshTokenExpiresAt: timestamp("refresh_token_expires_at"),
    scope: text("scope"),
    password: text("password"),
    createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow().notNull(),
    updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at")
      .$onUpdate(() => /* @__PURE__ */ new Date())
      .notNull(),
  },
  (table) => [index("account_userId_idx").on(table.userId)],
);

export const verification = pgTable(
  "verification",
  {
    id: text("id").primaryKey(),
    identifier: text("identifier").notNull(),
    value: text("value").notNull(),
    expiresAt: timestamp("expires_at").notNull(),
    createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow().notNull(),
    updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at")
      .defaultNow()
      .$onUpdate(() => /* @__PURE__ */ new Date())
      .notNull(),
  },
  (table) => [index("verification_identifier_idx").on(table.identifier)],
);

docs:
## Overview

`auth` installs the app-level Better Auth foundation used by the sign-in, sign-up, and user-button blocks. It includes the server config, React client, Drizzle auth tables, and the TanStack Start API route that forwards `/api/auth/*` requests to Better Auth.

## Included Files

- `lib/auth.ts` - Better Auth server setup with the Drizzle adapter
- `lib/auth-client.ts` - Better Auth React client
- `db/auth-schema.ts` - Generated Better Auth Drizzle tables
- `routes/api/$.tsx` - TanStack Start server route for `/api/auth/*`

## Additional Setup

Add the auth tables to your app's `db/schema.ts` barrel, merging them with any existing tables:

```ts
import * as authSchema from "./auth-schema";

export const schema = {
  ...authSchema,
};
```

This block uses the Better Auth Drizzle `relations-v2` adapter and currently needs this package override:

```json
{
  "overrides": {
    "@better-auth/drizzle-adapter": "https://pkg.pr.new/better-auth/better-auth/@better-auth/drizzle-adapter@6913"
  }
}
```

## Usage

```tsx
import { authClient } from "@/lib/auth-client";

const { data: session } = authClient.useSession();
```

Server code can import the configured auth instance:

```ts
import { auth } from "@/lib/auth";
```

## Environment Variables

- `DATABASE_URL` - PostgreSQL connection string used by the database service
- `BETTER_AUTH_SECRET` - Secret used by Better Auth
- `BETTER_AUTH_URL` - Public auth URL, for example `http://localhost:3000`
- `BETTER_AUTH_TRUSTED_ORIGINS` - Optional comma-separated list of trusted origins
